approvalBoundary: Approves the repository-runnable patient account, records, communications, support entry, support workspace, masking, replay-safe fallback, and patient-support parity baseline. It does not approve credentialled live NHS login, live provider replay, production clinical-safety signoff, DSPT signoff, or operational deployment readiness.

mandatorySuites.label: Patient, support, and record-artifact continuity suite
mandatorySuites.summary: The definitive cross-cutting continuity suite passes with machine-readable case results, accessibility evidence, screenshot evidence, trace evidence, and explicit remediation of repository-owned defects discovered during the suite buildout.

gateQuestions.question: Are tasks 210 to 224 complete, source-traceable, and internally coherent?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. The checklist marks 210 through 224 complete, the conformance rows bind each capability family to owning tasks and source references, and the merge/seam log reports no unresolved cross-cutting gap.

gateQuestions.question: Did the definitive continuity suite from 224 pass with machine-readable evidence?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. Seq 224 reports overall passed status, no blocked external cases, explicit family-level pass status, and resolved repository-owned defects captured in the defect log.

gateQuestions.question: Do patient account, records, communications, support entry, support workspace, masking, and fallback routes now behave like one coherent product family?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. The approved rows cover all required route families and bind them to one same-shell, continuity-preserving, evidence-first product grammar rather than separate local route semantics.

gateQuestions.question: Are patient and support surfaces demonstrably aligned to the same Phase 2 identity, status, and capability truth?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. Seq 223 publishes one shared identity and status bundle, resolves the earlier merge seams, and the browser-visible parity suite confirms the same cause classes and continuity labels across patient and support routes.

gateQuestions.question: Are record artifacts and communication artifacts parity-safe and restriction-safe?
gateQuestions.answer: Yes. Record parity, release gating, visualization fallback, communication visibility, and receipt alignment are all bound to the same current artifact and status truth, and the continuity suite verifies the restricted and read-only variants in browser-visible form.

gateQuestions.question: Which evidence is repository-runnable today, and which later live-environment proofs still remain outside this baseline?
gateQuestions.answer: All nine conformance rows are backed by repository-runnable artifacts. Two carry-forward items remain explicitly outside this baseline: credentialled live-provider replay proof and production clinical, security, and operational signoff.

gateQuestions.question: Which items are intentionally deferred to Phase 3 tasks 226+, and why are they not blockers for the current exit?
gateQuestions.answer: The triage workspace contract pack, queue and duplicate fairness pack, and endpoint decision and escalation pack are explicitly deferred to 226 through 229. They are not blockers because the current gate approves only the patient/support baseline, not the later human-checkpoint write surfaces built on top of it.

contradictionChecks.summary: The exit pack approves only surfaces that consume the already-frozen Phase 2 identity, status, and recovery truth rather than redefining it locally.
Support ticket, controlled resend, observe, and replay routes remain bound to the patient-visible delivery and repair truth instead of forming a second authoritative channel.
Record summaries, charts, documents, and communication previews fail closed into governed fallback modes without implying unsupported source authority or visibility.
The approved verdict is explicitly scoped to the repository-runnable baseline and keeps live-provider and production-assurance proof outside the current boundary.
